pi_items.source: Downloaded from https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/__moin_import__/attachments/SampleCaptures/200722_tcp_anon.pcapng and verified filename and format (pcapng version 1.0)
Verified in task.json description and export_result.sh script (lines 19-25)
Downloaded and measured actual file size; matches evidence_docs/README.md line 233
tshark analysis of actual PCAP file; matches evidence_docs/README.md line 233
tshark filter comparison: all 35 packets pass TCP filter (-Y tcp), confirming 100% TCP protocol composition
tshark endpoints statistics (endpoints,ip): 192.168.200.135 has 10,629 Tx Bytes vs 192.168.200.21 with 894 Tx Bytes. Matches evidence_docs/README.md line 165-166
tshark field extraction (ip.src, ip.len): 192.168.200.135 sent 10,309 bytes vs 192.168.200.21 with 670 bytes. Matches setup_task.sh ground truth calculation method
tshark endpoints statistics shows exactly 2 unique IPv4 addresses: 192.168.200.135 and 192.168.200.21
evidence_docs/README.md line 7 and line 226 confirm: 'real network captures from the official Wireshark SampleCaptures wiki page', not synthetic
Install script (install_wireshark.sh line 96) specifies fallback URLs; successfully downloaded from primary URL

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official Wireshark sample capture file '200722_tcp_anon.pcapng' (12,800 bytes, 35 TCP packets, real network traffic from wiki.wireshark.org). The file contains traffic between two IPv4 endpoints: 192.168.200.135 (top sender with 10,629 Tx Bytes) and 192.168.200.21 (minor sender with 894 Tx Bytes). The correct answer is 192.168.200.135. The task requires saving this IP to 'top_talker.txt'.
